_comment: gears-rust × GPT-5.3-codex-spark open-issue bug-fix bake-off. Numbers come from .context/gears-spark-bakeoff-log.md and the live traces under .artifacts/gears-spark/. Render with the slidey VS Code extension or `slidey bundle`; do NOT commit the .slidey.html.

meta.title: Can a cheap, fast model fix real OPEN bugs in kitsoki?

theme.name: rose-pine-moon
theme.fontFamily: ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, Helvetica Neue, Arial, sans-serif

scenes.title: GPT-5.3-codex-spark on gears-rust
scenes.subtitle: Driving the kitsoki bugfix pipeline LIVE over real OPEN issues
scenes.narration: Can a cheap, fast model — GPT five point three codex spark — fix real, still-open bugs in a large Rust monorepo, when it's driven through kitsoki's structured bug-fix pipeline? We tested it live.

scenes.body: Prior bake-offs graded against the regression test a merged PR shipped. Here we point the pipeline at issues that are still OPEN at HEAD — no maintainer fix to copy. We grade with a hidden public-API oracle where one exists, and open the resulting fix as an autonomous, clearly-unreviewed PR.
scenes.narration: The twist this time: we worked on issues that are still open. No merged fix to lean on. Where a hidden oracle exists we grade against it; otherwise the fix goes out as an autonomous, explicitly-unreviewed pull request for a human to check.

scenes.title: The setup
scenes.narration: gears-rust is a large, mature Rust monorepo. The worker model is GPT five point three codex spark, chosen at session time by a kitsoki harness profile. Every cell is driven head-less over the kitsoki studio M-C-P — a cheap Sonnet orchestrator only clicks the pipeline forward; the spark model does all the real work inside the session.

cards.label: gears-rust
cards.sub: large mature Rust monorepo · private fork

scenes.title: Open-issue triage — what's actually fixable

cells: localized: one fn in toolkit config; has a hidden oracle
process/infra tooling, not a code bug

scenes.caption: Most open issues are epics. The honest single-bug backlog: 4115 (clean) and 4052 (stretch).

scenes.title: 4115 — solved by spark
scenes.narration: Issue forty-one fifteen: a gear named with dashes can't be overridden by a Kubernetes env var, because env names can't carry dashes. Spark reproduced it RED, wrote a one-file fix that remaps the gear-name segment, and landed it. The hidden oracle went green, three hundred fifteen library tests stayed green, and the approach matches what a human maintainer would write.

cards.label: RED reproducer
cards.sub: committed, bug confirmed live at HEAD

scenes.title: Results
scenes.caption: Spark: 1 solved, 1 pending (quota, NOT a capability result). 4052 landed under GPT-5.5 — 2 autonomous PRs total.

scenes.body: F2 (real bug, fixed): host.local.run_tests fork/exec'd test_cmd directly, so an env-prefixed command (CARGO_TARGET_DIR=… cargo test) failed 'no such file or directory' and bounced the testing gate to idle. Now leading NAME=VALUE tokens route through /usr/bin/env — POSIX semantics, no shell. Regression-tested, committed to main. F1 (minor): import-prefixed intents emit a false-alarm validation_failed before resolving — documented as polish.
scenes.narration: Driving real bugs surfaced a real kitsoki bug. The pipeline's test step fork-exec'd the test command directly, so an environment-prefixed command failed outright and bounced the testing gate. We fixed it to route environment prefixes through env — proper Unix semantics, no shell — with a regression test, committed to main. Dogfooding paid for itself.

scenes.lede: Cheap model, real fix — when it's in budget
scenes.body: Inside kitsoki's reproduce-first, oracle-graded pipeline, GPT-5.3-codex-spark produced a correct, maintainer-grade fix for a real open bug in 2 turns. The honest caveat: its usage cap is low — one good fix exhausted the daily quota, so a thin-budget model needs cell-level pacing or fallback. The pipeline wiring stayed healthy throughout, and the bug it exposed made kitsoki better.
scenes.narration: The takeaway: inside kitsoki's reproduce-first, oracle-graded pipeline, even a cheap fast model produced a maintainer-grade fix for a real open bug in two turns. The caveat is budget — one good fix exhausted spark's daily quota, so a thin-budget model needs pacing or fallback. And the dogfood made kitsoki itself more robust.

scenes.title: Scoreboard
scenes.narration: Two real open bugs fixed and shipped as autonomous, clearly-unreviewed pull requests. One real kitsoki bug found and fixed. Both fixes independently verified — green oracle for one, a revert-to-red proof for the other.
